signals.reasoning: This fresh SOL short is slightly green and sitting at its current peak unrealized P&L, so I would manage it as a hold rather than cover it immediately. Confidence is still capped because the broader market structure is not cleanly bearish and the position still has no stop with only about 1% liquidation distance.
BTC still looks structurally firm after the breakout and is not a clean fresh short. It stays watch-only while the live SOL position is managed.
ETH remains one of the stronger structures on the board and does not offer a cleaner bearish edge than the live SOL trade. It stays on watch.
GRIFFAIN looks like a completed long-liquidation flush rather than an easy fresh short. I would not chase it lower from here.
CC has squeeze-long support from negative funding and constructive price action, but the setup is not cleaner than managing the live SOL position and the supporting OI history is incomplete.
ZEREBRO has upside momentum, but it is already extended and not clean enough to justify action in a position-management pass.
MERL still looks constructive with price and open interest both up, which makes it a poor fresh short and not urgent enough to distract from the live SOL management decision.
GRASS is already in a liquidation-style flush and does not look early enough to press fresh. It stays sidelined.

HEMI is the clearest secondary short lean because price is slightly down while open interest is still building, which points to fresh shorts pressing rather than a finished flush. Confidence stays moderate because the broader tape still has residual squeeze support.
LDO remains a strong continuation name, but it is too extended to chase and too strong structurally to fade casually. Watch-only.
TST is still a crowded squeeze continuation and does not offer a clean new edge either way from this point.
